mapping.a_element: the 2D Ising fixed point about which the RG flow is expanded
mapping.b_element: the asymptotically-free (continuum-limit) point of the O(2)/Sine-Gordon model

mapping.a_element: quenched disorder acting as a marginally irrelevant perturbation
mapping.b_element: the marginal (asymptotically free) coupling / operator controlling the approach to the continuum limit

mapping.a_element: renormalization-group equations for the marginal coupling, whose slow logarithmic running drives the corrections
mapping.b_element: the logarithmically running coupling near the asymptotically free point

mapping.a_element: logarithmic corrections to the scaling of thermodynamic quantities
mapping.b_element: leading lattice artifacts that vanish only as inverse powers of the logarithm of the correlation length

mapping.a_element: correlation length / system size in the finite-size scaling analysis
mapping.b_element: the correlation length whose logarithm sets the size of the artifacts

mapping.a_element: universality of the logarithmic corrections due to disorder
mapping.b_element: universality of the leading lattice artifacts

brief_justification: Both are governed by the same RG machinery: a marginal operator in a two-dimensional renormalization-group flow whose logarithmic running yields universal corrections that decay only as inverse powers of a logarithm rather than as a power law. In A the marginally irrelevant disorder produces universal logarithmic corrections to the 2D Ising critical behavior; in B the marginal, asymptotically-free coupling produces universal lattice artifacts vanishing as inverse powers of the log of the correlation length -- the same marginal-operator-driven log corrections play the identical role in each.
